steps.title: Identify Text 2's contradicting argument
steps.content: The underlined claim asserts that the UNESCO honor “is well known among both residents of the city and tourists who visit.” But Text 2 argues that without intentional marketing strategies, “many current and potential visitors to Bergamo may not even be aware” of the city's food scene—directly contradicting the assumption of widespread knowledge.

steps.title: Apply the contradiction to the choices
steps.content: The author of Text 2 responds to this claim by suggesting that the UNESCO status may not actually be well known among visitors, which is precisely why marketing strategies are necessary. A, C, and D miss the central point by defending the UNESCO honor itself, introducing unrelated benefits, or crediting chefs respectively. This matches choice B — B is correct.
